notes: Topic-grouped, BOUNDED corpus of verbatim Texas statute sections for civil + consumer-debt + family practice, fetched by scripts/pull_texas_statutes.py. The statutes.capitol.texas.gov viewer is a JavaScript single-page app; the verbatim per-chapter HTML it resolves to lives on the Legislature's file server at https://tcss.legis.texas.gov/resources/<CODE>/htm/<CODE>.<CHAPTER>.htm. The puller fetches each <CODE>.<CHAPTER> page once (cached), slices the HTML between section catchline anchors ('Sec. N.N.  TITLE.'), and writes each section's verbatim text under '## Tex. <Code> § N.N' headings. Code abbreviations: CP=Civil Practice & Remedies, FI=Finance, BC=Business & Commerce, PR=Property, FA=Family, GV=Government. Each topic file carries a single code; the chapter for each section is derived from the integer before the first dot. This is a representative set (the sections a drafter reaches for), not a full enumeration; Texas statutes are amended every regular and special legislative session, so verify currency before filing. A _file_is_stub guard keeps an offline run from clobbering committed text (pass --force to replace).

topics.title: Attorney fees, costs, judgments, and unsworn declarations
Texas Debt Collection Act (Finance Code ch. 392)
Deceptive Trade Practices–Consumer Protection Act (Bus. & Com. ch. 17)
Marriage, divorce, conservatorship, support, and maintenance
